authorization_stages.exact_path_rule: all eight paths absent before Stage2; create only these paths; modify no existing path
authorization_stages.stage: implementation and execution-ready real-production real-ZFS read-only preview

attestation_limits.reviewer_identity_source: transcript-observed dispatch task and result metadata, not user-provided identities

review_dispatch_provenance.evidence_kind: latest unique FINAL EXACT PROTOCOL REVIEW dispatch_parallel call and immediately following successful tool result

ancestor_and_dirfd_contract.abrain_event_surface: only the exact fixed-event S0-S4 FSM may write under /home/worker/.abrain
ancestor_and_dirfd_contract.ancestor_rule: open and verify every existing repository evidence ancestor and event ancestor with O_DIRECTORY|O_NOFOLLOW; reject symlink, non-directory, identity, owner, mode, device, inode, or expected-chain drift
ancestor_and_dirfd_contract.node_relative_path_mechanism: all repository creates and cleanup operations are relative to the retained verified evidence-dir FD through the Stage2-proven procfs dirfd surface; no unverified repository pathname reopen is permitted
ancestor_and_dirfd_contract.repository_write_surface: exact ratification, intent, and single terminal post-dossier paths only
ancestor_and_dirfd_contract.single_official_executor: one official Node Stage3 executor inside the verified confinement

artifact_bytes.file_bytes: UTF-8 RFC8785-JCS(object) plus exactly one LF; no BOM, CRLF, indentation, or extra whitespace
artifact_bytes.raw_sha256: lowercase SHA-256 over exact stored JCS+LF bytes
artifact_bytes.self_hash: lowercase SHA-256 over RFC8785-JCS of the same object with only its declared self-hash field omitted and without LF
artifact_bytes.verification: parse, omit only the declared self field, recompute self hash, require byte-exact JCS+LF reserialization, then recompute raw hash

bootstrap.C0a_C0b: before intent, compare exact equality of every closed stable anchor enumerated below; embed the resulting C0 object in intent
bootstrap.C1: after durable intent and before any event mutation, recheck stable anchors and initial Stage3 authorization exact/latest/fresh
bootstrap.Ccommit: immediately before link(temp,target), recheck stable anchors and initial Stage3 authorization exact/latest/fresh
bootstrap.I0: original I0 plus final ratification, absent intent, absent target and event temp, and exact prestate; only I0 creates intent
bootstrap.drift_rule: if a completed pre-link check actually observes drift, do not append

closed_stable_anchors: exact genesis plus beee proposition event coordinate, bytes, counts, selected/foldable zero
fixed target, first-shard identity, second-shard/event-temp/target state, and no-symlink event ancestor chain
registry bytes, schema/binding anchors, and generic L1_SCHEMA_WRITE_DISABLED gate
P2a bundle/latest exact bytes and inventory
P2b.1 plan/dossier exact bytes, blocked status, no production destination, and runtime unreachability
verified Stage2 implementation source closure, executable/loader/DSO/JCS/bwrap/procfs/flock closure, package/runtime roots, and protected stable surfaces
Stage3 authorization coordinate, exact bytes, transcript prefix, and continuous chain
authorization_checkpoints.whole_abrain_inventory: capture a no-follow raw inventory with regular-file byte hashes and lstat/readlink symlink observations as evidence only; it is not a C0/C1/Ccommit gate

exact_fsync_chain: S0 mkdir exact 8c mode 0700 then fsync 1c; EEXIST target-first reclassifies
S1 or cleaned S2 create exact event temp O_EXCL|O_NOFOLLOW mode 0600, enforce identity/mode, write exact envelope, fsync temp
complete Ccommit immediately before hardlink no-replace publication
link event temp to target; verify same dev/ino, nlink2, owner, mode, and exact bytes; refsync file and fsync 8c
unlink only exact recognized event temp; fsync 8c
S4 verify target-only nlink1, fsync target and 8c, exact readback/restat and poststate checks
event_fsm.scanner: retain the frozen Round6 scanner and deterministic event-temp grammar; no scanner or shard deletion change

states.S0: target absent, exact event temp absent, 8c absent; validate 1c and create only retained 8c
states.S1: target and event temp absent; 8c exact, empty, executor-owned, non-symlink, mode 0700; adopt and never delete it
states.S2: target absent and sole 8c entry is exact event temp; with valid intent and held kernel lock, require regular owner/mode identity, unlink it regardless of partial/exact bytes, fsync, and recreate fresh
states.S3: target plus exact event temp only; require same dev/ino, both nlink2, exact bytes/owner/mode, then unlink temp, fsync, require target nlink1, and continue to S4
states.S4: target only; require no-follow regular exact owner/mode/bytes/nlink1, complete refsync/readback/poststate and terminal post handling

kernel_lock.acquire: after verified ancestor/evidence-dir opens and confinement setup, before ratification/intent/post inspection or creation and before every abrain check; map the retained lock-directory OFD to child fd 3 and execute pinned /usr/bin/flock -xn 3 from the verified execution closure
kernel_lock.busy_semantics: child status 1 with no signal means BUSY; contender returns immediately and performs no target, authorization, stable-anchor, transcript, or post-dossier checks
kernel_lock.environment: scrub child environment and cwd; inherit only fd 3 and the minimum verified stdio/null descriptors; all other child FDs default closed
kernel_lock.hard_failure_semantics: any other exit status, signal, spawn error, executable/loader/DSO/procfs/FD identity anomaly, or syscall anomaly is a hard failure
kernel_lock.holder_lifetime: status 0 with no signal means acquired; parent Node keeps the same OFD open, children do not inherit it by default, and closing it or SIGKILL of the holder releases the kernel lock
kernel_lock.lock_artifacts: none: no lock file, PID, mtime, token, owner record, stale recovery, dead-owner recovery, or token test
kernel_lock.scope: flock coordinates only official executors that use this exact protocol
kernel_lock.supersedes_round6_lock: the Round6 dedicated PID/mtime/token lock and its contention/stale/dead/token tests are retired

mutation_accounting.first_level_metadata_side_effect: 1c may gain only mtime/ctime advancement and one directory link for retained 8c; owner/mode/dev/ino/realpath/type/non-symlink remain exact

observation_boundary.incomplete_claim: terminal incomplete may claim only drift actually observed by a completed check in this run
not_claimed: transient drift that reverted before a completed check
drift not durably observed before SIGKILL
observation_boundary.post_durability: later live drift does not retroactively change an exact self-valid terminal COMPLETE or INCOMPLETE artifact
observation_boundary.sigkill_rule: make no assertion beyond durable evidence that existed before process death

post_terminal_protocol.complete_binding: exact self-valid COMPLETE statically binds exact ratification, intent, fixed target tuple, and the recorded clean S4 observation
post_terminal_protocol.incomplete_binding: exact self-valid target_durable_evidence_incomplete statically binds exact ratification, intent, attempted tuple/target, and the completed-check observed-drift basis
post_terminal_protocol.malformed_or_foreign: hard fail without overwrite or cleanup
post_terminal_protocol.no_overwrite: COMPLETE and target_durable_evidence_incomplete share one final path, are mutually exclusive, and neither can overwrite the other
precedence_after_target_first: exact self-valid COMPLETE => verify/identical and stop
exact self-valid target_durable_evidence_incomplete => terminal incomplete and stop
absent => current checks decide no-replace COMPLETE or target_durable_evidence_incomplete creation
malformed, foreign, byte-different, or differently bound => hard fail

recovery.authorization_rule: C1/Ccommit/S4 recovery requires the recorded authorization coordinate, exact bytes, prefix, and continuous parent chain intact plus transcript suffix append-only; it does not require latest-user or time freshness
recovery.exact_target_with_observed_drift: if post is absent, write terminal target_durable_evidence_incomplete; if post exists apply terminal precedence
recovery.foreign_target: hard fail with no replacement or cleanup
order: classify target first as absent, exact durable, or foreign
then check closed stable anchors and recovery authorization contract
recovery.target_absent_with_observed_drift: do not append and fail; because target is not durable, do not create target_durable_evidence_incomplete
recovery.target_absent_without_drift: continue only through recovery C1 and Ccommit checks before no-replace event publication

repo_evidence_no_replace.common_create: derive same-directory temp deterministically from expected content raw SHA-256; open relative to verified evidence dirfd with O_CREAT|O_EXCL|O_NOFOLLOW mode 0600; enforce and verify owner/mode/dev/ino/nlink; write/readback exact bytes; file fsync; hardlink temp to final without replace; verify same dev/ino and nlink2; fsync parent; unlink recognized temp; fsync parent; require final nlink1; refsync/readback final and parent
repo_evidence_no_replace.final_absent_sole_temp_recovery: only the sole deterministic recognized temp is eligible; require regular no-follow expected owner/mode and nlink1, unlink, fsync parent, prove absence, then recreate fresh
repo_evidence_no_replace.final_and_temp_recovery: accept cleanup only when final and temp are same dev/ino, both nlink2, exact owner/mode/type and exact expected bytes; then unlink temp, fsync parent, require final nlink1, and reverify
repo_evidence_no_replace.final_exact_alone: require exact self-valid expected bytes, owner/mode/type/dev/ino and nlink1; refsync/readback and return identical
repo_evidence_no_replace.other_state: fail closed with no replacement and no foreign cleanup

reversal.cancellation_or_remediation: requires separate design, review, and fresh authorization
reversal.semantic_retraction: requires separately authorized proposition lifecycle append

required_proofs: pinned /usr/bin/flock execution closure and same-OFD lock lifetime
all repo-evidence/event fsync and hardlink semantics
stage2_acceptance.required_real_environment: real production data and the real production ZFS filesystem only
stage2_acceptance.unsupported_outcome: fail Stage2, generate no authorization text, and provide no fallback, substitute filesystem, synthetic fixture, handwritten evidence, or non-ZFS acceptance

supersession_and_history.earlier_round3: the earlier Round-3 shared random-temp direct event-target plan remains superseded by Round6 S0-S4
supersession_and_history.round13_replacement: Round13 supersedes the Round6 dedicated PID/mtime/token lock and tightens bootstrap/recovery, repo-evidence publication, terminal precedence, and observation-bounded claims
round6_preserved: target-first S0-S4 classifier and scanner agreement
no shard deletion and exact mutation accounting
supersession_and_history.round6_status: historical accepted Round-6 six-vendor unanimous SIGN architecture milestone

threat_boundary.flock_limit: advisory flock coordinates official executors only and is not claimed to exclude foreign writers
threat_boundary.foreign_change: no-replace, inode/link identity, stable-anchor, ancestor, and exact-byte checks fail closed; an official executor cannot replace or damage foreign bytes
